title: Bridge Essay: Spatial Distance and Temporal Attentional Focus Modulate Voluntary Action Prepar

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nested Agency** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Agency as inference — the 'I' as nested prediction error minimization, not homunculus.

**Spatial Distance and Temporal Attentional Focus Modulate Voluntary Action Preparation and Awareness.**. Psychophysiology.. PMID: 41823074. DOI: 10.1111/psyp.70280. Peripersonal space (PPS)-the immediate space surrounding the body-modulates perception and motor control. However, its impact on how voluntary actions are initiated and subjectively experienced remains underexplored. Similarly, how directing attention to different phases of an action, such as intention formation versus execution, and anticipating outcomes of an action, modulates the neural readiness for movement, has yet to be fully examined.
